pipeline_status.poller: QUIET — 11th consecutive quiet poll. No dome site changes since 2026-04-04.
pipeline_status.analyst: Globe fingerprint hunt active — 33/67 complete. WIN-033 (Sigma Octantis): confirmed no globe constant, entirely qualitative. No completed expansions awaiting integration.
pipeline_status.curmudgeon: Cycle 2, Phase 1: 36/67 WINs reviewed (WIN-001 through WIN-036). 31 pending (WIN-037 through WIN-067). All sections and holistic checks pending.

external_changes.dome_site_changes: No changes detected. 11th consecutive quiet poll. All dome parameters stable (disc_radius=20015 km, firmament=H(r)=8537*exp(-r/8619), sun=5733 km, moon=2534 km, accuracy=95.2%). ECM V51.0 unchanged.

internal_issues.description: SEC-3.5.4 tidal WIN paragraph lacked specific date and timing context for 5 WINs added on 2026-04-05, hours before review launch.

suggested_patch.find: registered on the same day — a batch expansion of the WIN count using well-established constants that add no discriminating power
suggested_patch.replace: registered on 2026-04-05 — hours before this critical review was launched — a batch expansion of the WIN count using well-established constants that add no discriminating power. Strategic timing: adopting nineteenth-century tidal constants as new "predictions" on the eve of an independent audit raises the question of whether these WINs were added to inflate the count rather than because the dome model genuinely predicts them

internal_issues.description: SEC-3.5.4 showed absolute count for relabels_standard but not proportional significance.

suggested_patch.find: of {{TOTAL_WINS}} WINs relabel standard physics as "aetheric" phenomena — the single most common structural pattern in the model
suggested_patch.replace: of {{TOTAL_WINS}} WINs (nearly three-quarters of all claims) relabel standard physics as "aetheric" phenomena — the single most common structural pattern in the model

internal_issues.description: Verdict legend ordered arbitrarily. Reordered by evidential force: Self-Contradicted > Refuted > Standard > Misleading > Not Demonstrated > Unfalsifiable.
Not Demonstrated definition expanded to cover post-hoc fits and undemonstrated mechanisms, not just replication failures.
KILLSHOT-GAIA scale comparison already existed: '4.7 billion times the dome maximum firmament height'. Already fixed.
No 'space telescope' wording found in KILLSHOT-GAIA section. Already fixed or never present.
WIN-064 code_analysis=null footnote — WIN-064 now has reviewed=true code_analysis tags. Already fixed.
WIN-058 verdict mismatch — WIN-058 already has verdict='Not Demonstrated' and appropriately framed verdict text. Already fixed by prior run.

SEC-3.1 cross-reference and code_analysis stats — both already present: Tesla patent cross-ref exists in Domain #2 row; code_analysis stats use {{CA_REVIEWED}}/{{CA_DOME}} placeholders with Part 4.6 link. Already fixed.
SEC-3.1 never explains what λ_g was fitted to. Needs analyst research into H(r) curve fitting with 2 free parameters and statistical independence argument rigor.
SEC-3.2 cross-reference mismatch: Kill-Shot Test 5 references eclipse PATH but 3.2 discusses magnetic anomaly. Needs analyst to restructure SEC-3.2.
SEC-3.2: '0.0 nT exactly' globe prediction quote needs verification against dome site content. Assigned to EXP-004 for analyst verification.
WIN-024 structural inconsistency in SEC-3.5.4: section treats it as relabeling but relabels_standard=false. Assigned to EXP-009 for prose restructuring.
DOCX missing Sections 5.14-5.16 — requires changes to build-doc-v4.js. Pending human developer intervention.
Dome source files (inject_ai_layer.py, update_optical_caveats.py) not archived in repo. Requires human to add files to downloads/ or link to GitHub.
DOCX Self-Contradicted count hardcoded as 11 in build-doc-v4.js. Requires developer to update build script to compute count dynamically.
KILLSHOT-GAIA section missing from DOCX/PDF. Requires addition to build-doc-v4.js.
